title: acmella affinis
intro: Acmella affinis, also known as the toothache plant, is a flowering plant species in the family Asteraceae. It is used in traditional medicine due to its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids. The plant's medicinal properties have been found to provide health benefits, including pain relief, reduced inflammation, and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential treatment for oral health issues and wound healing. Acmella affinis is often used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall health.
